title: StanceExtractionResult
description: Output produced by the stance extractor for a single news article. Contains zero-to-many atomic stance events.

doc_id.description: Unique identifier of the source article (matches news_articles.doc_id).

stance_events.description: Zero or more atomic stance events extracted from the article.

StanceEvent.title: StanceEvent
StanceEvent.description: One atomic stance event: a single politician expressing or enacting a single proposition.

politician.description: Full name of the politician taking the stance.

topic.description: High-level policy area. Must be one of the controlled vocabulary values.

subtopic.description: Optional finer-grained sub-category within the topic.

normalized_proposition.description: A single declarative sentence summarising the politician's position in plain language.

stance_direction.description: The direction of the stance toward the proposition.

stance_mode.description: The mode or form through which the stance is expressed.

speaker.description: Who delivered the statement. May differ from politician (e.g. a spokesperson). Optional.

target_entity.description: The entity the stance is directed at (a person, country, bill, organisation, etc.). Optional.

event_date.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD) when the stance was expressed.

event_date_precision.description: How precise event_date is. Must be provided when event_date is set.

evidence_role.description: How the supporting evidence relates to the stance.

quote_text.description: Verbatim text from the article that supports this event. Optional.

quote_start_char.description: 0-based character offset of quote_text in the article body.

quote_end_char.description: Exclusive end character offset of quote_text in the article body.

paraphrase.description: A brief paraphrase of the supporting evidence in plain language.

confidence.description: Extractor confidence in this event (0.0 = no confidence, 1.0 = certain).

notes.description: Extraction caveats, uncertainty notes, or edge-case explanations.
